statute: No statewide GC license law; local municipalities govern. C.R.S. § 12-58 (plumbing/electrical licensing)
license_notes: Colorado has no statewide GC license requirement. Plumbing and electrical contractors must hold state licenses. Denver, Colorado Springs, and other municipalities have their own GC licensing requirements — check locally.

mandatory_clauses.label: Contractor information
Local licensing note
mandatory_clauses.text: IMPORTANT: Colorado does not require a statewide general contractor license, but local municipalities may have their own requirements. Contractor represents that they hold all required local licenses and permits for the jurisdiction where work is performed.

mandatory_clauses.label: Mechanics lien notice
mandatory_clauses.text:
NOTICE OF LIEN RIGHTS

Under state law, contractors, subcontractors, and material suppliers who provide labor or materials for your property and are not paid may have the right to file a mechanic's lien against your property. Even if you pay your general contractor in full, you may still be subject to liens from unpaid subcontractors and suppliers. Protect yourself by obtaining written lien waivers from all parties before making final payment.

mandatory_clauses.label: Federal 3-day right to cancel (TILA)
Not legal advice disclaimer
mandatory_clauses.text: DISCLAIMER: This contract was generated by ContractorDesk as a starting point template. It does not constitute legal advice. Contractors are encouraged to have a licensed attorney review agreements for their specific situation and applicable state requirements.

penalties_for_non_compliance: Plumbing/electrical unlicensed = Class 2 misdemeanor (first offense), Class 6 felony (subsequent). Municipal penalties vary.
notes: No statewide GC license. Denver requires licensing through Community Planning and Development. All businesses must register with the state. Plumbing and electrical require state licenses. AI-generated contracts are explicitly recognized as valid in Colorado.
